extraction.one_line_summary: WELLENS Tim won from a 12-rider breakaway after two riders separated in the finale, sprinting past ARENSMAN Thymen who had gone earlier.
tactical_patterns: WELLENS Tim's 29 career wins gave composure advantage over 20-year-old ARENSMAN Thymen in two-up sprint despite similar form.
Sunweb placed three riders in 12-man break, creating numerical advantage that allowed ARENSMAN to attack with WELLENS late.
ROGLIČ Primož gained 4 seconds on GC rivals by sprinting peloton finish despite 2:13 gap to break, tactical acceleration before crash.
Three-kilometer crash rule confusion suggests future similar finishes require positioning before final descent complexities, not after.
KUSS Sepp became virtual race leader mid-stage from breakaway, demonstrating GC vulnerability when multiple top-10 teams commit riders forward.

rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: Stage win plus KOM jersey shows peak form after Tour de France non-selection, now motivated to defend mountains lead.
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: positive: First Grand Tour rider at 20 years old nearly won stage after late separation, inexperience cost sprint finish.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Accelerated peloton finish to gain 4 seconds on GC rivals despite stage won 2:13 ahead, alert tactical racing.
rider_signals.GROßSCHARTNER Felix: positive: Only lost 1 second to ROGLIČ at peloton finish while other GC contenders lost 4, positioned better through chaos.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Rode tempo in 12-man break containing multiple top-10 teams, virtual leader role shows GC ambitions remain viable.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Lost 4 seconds to ROGLIČ at finish, but 10 KOM points available next stage could reclaim mountains jersey from WELLENS.
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: neutral: Avoided mechanicals unlike previous stage but lost 4 seconds to ROGLIČ, positioning through finale descent still problematic.
